rationale: Both correctly diagnose the config/tokenizer mismatch and recommend the same DiT-config workaround that FayeSpica confirmed, matching the thread's resolution. X is cleaner and more thoroughly grounded — it cites more precise line ranges, quotes the surrounding init code, notes the downstream `_all_ratio_ids` usage, and adds a plausible verifiable observation (both YAML headers say 'Instruct', misleading for DiT) plus properly frames the Gaohan123 follow-up-issue request. Y introduces an unverified/likely-fabricated detail ('VAE resolution bins' for the extra ratio tokens) not supported by any cited source, and its output is marred by raw, duplicated agent-report scaffolding (repeated draft_answer blocks, internal 'findings/assumptions/blockers' metadata) that isn't fit to post as a maintainer comment, hurting completeness and polish.
